title: Box Office Round-Up: Civil War Claims a Surprising Victory Over Abigail, Newcomers Fail to Capture Top Spot
text: Abigail opened with a 3-day domestic box office gross of $10 million and a global opening of $15.2 million. The R-rated horror film had been projected to make between $12 million to $15 million in its opening weekend, so the actual results came in slightly below expectations. Abigail was forecast to be the weekend's winner but only made $10.2 million, finishing in second place. Civil War held onto first place with $11.1 million, a drop of 57% from its record-setting opening for A24. The film, directed by Matt Bettinelli-Olpin and Tyler Gillett, cost $28 million, and as long as inaugural revenues land on the higher end of estimates, Abigail shouldn't struggle to turn a profit in its theatrical run. The film came into the weekend with great reviews at 82% on Rotten Tomatoes. However, audiences gave the movie a not-great B CinemaScore. Its $1 million in previews was a bad sign that audiences just weren't aware or interested that the title character was an adorable but murderous little girl vampire. The film ultimately grossed $43 million on its $28 million production budget.
